nodes.label: MCP server
nodes.description: mcp_server.py — exposes 8 brAIn graph tools to Claude Code via the Model Context Protocol, registered globally in ~/.claude/settings.json so every Claude Code session in any working directory has native graph access without any project-level setup. Uses mcp.server.stdio transport. Reads the graph read_only=True by default; only brain_ingest opens a read-write connection. Mirrors brain.py CLI output via formatter functions (_fmt_find, _fmt_show, _fmt_chain, _fmt_paths, _fmt_stats) so the MCP and CLI surfaces are consistent. 8 tools: brain_find, brain_show, brain_causes, brain_effects, brain_paths, brain_stats, brain_query, brain_ingest.

nodes.label: MCP global registration
nodes.description: The MCP server is registered under ~/.claude/settings.json (user-level, not project-level). Consequence: every Claude Code session — regardless of working directory — inherits the 8 brain_* tools without any per-project configuration. This is the primary interface through which Claude interacts with the graph during day-to-day work, as opposed to the FastAPI backend which is only active when the React UI is running.

nodes.label: React UI
nodes.description: ui/frontend/ — React 18 + Vite frontend running on port 5173. Three panels: (1) graph canvas with force-directed layout via @xyflow/react and d3-force, nodes scaled by importance and colored by type; (2) sidebar with live search, type-filter badges, and node list; (3) node detail panel showing description, relations with evidence, confidence scores, and sources. Also includes an Ingest panel (modal) for triggering autonomous project ingestion from the UI.

nodes.label: FastAPI backend
nodes.description: ui/backend/main.py — FastAPI server on port 8000, backing the React UI. Endpoints: GET /api/graph (full graph for canvas), GET /api/node/{id} (node detail + neighbors), GET /api/search (label/description substring + type filter), GET /api/types (node type distribution), GET /api/stats, GET /api/audit, POST /api/ingest (starts ingestion job), GET /api/ingest/{job_id} (polls job status and live output). Kuzu opened read_only=True for all query endpoints; ingestion goes through the claude CLI subprocess, not direct Kuzu writes.

nodes.label: Autonomous ingestion
nodes.description: When a user submits a project name + folder path via the React UI, the FastAPI backend spawns a dedicated claude --print --dangerously-skip-permissions subprocess (daemon thread, non-blocking). The subprocess receives a 6-step INGEST_PROMPT referencing SKILL.md: list .md files, extract each following the section-inventory → entity-pass → relation-pass → completeness-review protocol, save JSON payloads to projects/<name>/, run brain.py ingest, verify with stats + audit, then run gap verification per doc. Output is streamed line-by-line into _jobs[job_id][output] and polled by the frontend every 2 seconds. The spawned session has no prior context — its sole purpose is extraction.

rels.evidence: mcp_server.py is a core component of brAIn — it is the primary interface through which Claude Code sessions access and update the graph in normal use.
Without global registration in ~/.claude/settings.json, the MCP server would need per-project configuration — the global registration is what makes the graph available in every session without setup.

The MCP tools (brain_find, brain_show, etc.) need to be called intentionally. CLAUDE.md provides the session-start protocol and extraction guidelines that tell Claude when and how to use those tools — without it, the tools exist but are not systematically used.
The React UI is the graphical interface of brAIn — it exposes graph exploration, search, and ingestion to users who prefer a visual interface over the CLI.
The React frontend fetches all data (graph, search, stats, ingestion status) from the FastAPI backend — the UI cannot function without the backend.
The autonomous ingestion mechanism is implemented in the FastAPI backend: POST /api/ingest + GET /api/ingest/{job_id} + the _run_ingestion thread function.

Both autonomous ingestion (UI path) and the MCP server (Claude Code path) ultimately call brain.py to persist the graph — they are two entry points to the same underlying operation, differing in initiator (user via UI vs Claude via tool call) and transport.

The MCP server's tool implementations call the same lib/ functions as brain.py (lib.query, lib.ingest, lib.db) — it depends on the same library layer, not brain.py the script directly, but they share the same plumbing.
